security_findings.title: Timing attack consideration noted but acceptable
security_findings.detail: Code comment at line 114-115 acknowledges timing attacks on bash string comparison. Reasoning is sound for file-based local bus. No issue.
security_findings.recommendation: Status: APPROVED — reasoning is documented and threat model is correct

quality_findings.title: Redundant secret check in bus_auth_sign_file
quality_findings.detail: Lines 185-189 re-check SECRET before signing, but bus_auth_sign already handles empty secret gracefully (returns empty string). This is defensive but adds slight cognitive load.
quality_findings.recommendation: Minor: Consider consolidating, but current approach is safe and explicit

security_findings.title: JIT-020: Sed injection vulnerability FIXED
security_findings.detail: Prior sed-based replacement with character escaping was fragile. Inputs with embedded newlines could inject additional sed commands or break the substitution. Python's str.replace() is literal (non-regex), eliminating injection surface.
security_findings.recommendation: STATUS: APPROVED — Excellent fix. Atomic write via tempfile ensures data consistency.

quality_findings.title: Python3 dependency introduced
quality_findings.detail: New code requires python3 (line 48). Check at line 45-48 errors gracefully if missing, which is good. But adds runtime dependency.
quality_findings.recommendation: Acceptable for security-critical path. Document in README or dependency manifest.

test_coverage.self_test: bus_auth_test() includes 6 test cases (sign, verify, tamper detection, wrong key, round-trip, strict unsigned rejection)
test_coverage.recommendation: Add integration test verifying bus rejects tampered messages in production flow

deployment_notes.rollback_path: Clear MANUSAT_BUS_SECRET to disable authentication (legacy mode)

rationale: Security fixes are well-motivated, implementation is solid with defensive programming patterns (atomic writes, explicit error handling). New HMAC authentication strengthens bus integrity. Single python3 dependency is acceptable for security-critical path. Recommend adding env var documentation to deployment guide.
next_steps: Deploy with MANUSAT_BUS_SECRET configured in production
Add integration test for tamper detection in CI
